title: taraxacum lancastriense
intro: Taraxacum lancastriense, a rare dandelion subspecies native to the north of England, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum lancastriense are attributed to its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain bioactive compounds such as inulin and sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and oxidative stress, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and prebiotic properties. Taraxacum lancastriense can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile remedy for promoting overall well-being.
